claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 15 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 15 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let me start by reading the official guidance on Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now let me start with the first question:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child - Alex, who's 15 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 15 years old.

Now I need to ask you a few questions to assess your eligibility:

**Do you live with Alex in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great. Now I need to ask:

**Are you responsible for Alex - meaning you provide for their care, such as food, clothes, and general living expenses?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for Alex - I provide for all their care, food, clothes, and everything they need.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Just a few more questions:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. A couple more questions:

**Is Alex currently in hospital, care, or residential accommodation?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. Actually, I should mention - I am fostering Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for sharing that information. This is important for determining your eligibility.

**Is the local council paying anything towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance as part of the fostering arrangement?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the local council doesn't pay anything towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me ask one final question:

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training (such as attending school)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is still in school - they're 15, so they're doing their GCSEs at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided, you meet all the eligibility criteria:

✓ Alex is 15 years old (under 16)
✓ You live with Alex in the UK
✓ You are responsible for Alex's care and provide for their needs
✓ You are fostering Alex and the local council is not paying anything towards their accommodation or maintenance
✓ Alex is in approved education (attending school)
✓ Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

According to the official guidance, you can get Child Benefit if you foster a child, as long as the local council is not paying anything towards their accommodation or maintenance - which applies to your situation.

You can now proceed to apply for Child Benefit. If you have any other questions about the application process, feel free to ask!

reasonings: Alex is 15 years old, which is under 16, so they meet the age requirement for Child Benefit. The claimant lives with Alex in the UK and is responsible for their care, providing food, clothes, and all their needs. The claimant is fostering Alex, and according to the eligibility rules, you can get Child Benefit if you foster a child as long as the local council is not paying anything towards their accommodation or maintenance. In this case, the local council is not paying anything towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is also in approved education (attending school and doing GCSEs). Therefore, the claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

The claimant is responsible for Alex's care and the local council does not pay towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. Alex is in school and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. The claimant meets all eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.
